# Rec Pass

> Rec Pass is a $199/year golf pass for Texas golfers: play any course in Texas, no limit. Partner courses are free (just show your pass); at any other course, Rec Pass covers your green fee up to $50 per round. Pre-launch; founding-member waitlist open.

## What Rec Pass is

Rec Pass is an annual golf membership that works at any public or municipal course in Texas — not a fixed network. One flat price, no per-course or per-round limit:

- **Partner (in-network) courses:** play free — just show your pass, no receipt.
- **Any other course:** play it and Rec Pass covers your green fee, up to $50 per round (weekday/off-peak, receipt required, paid after a quick review).
- No cap on how many courses or rounds you play.
- The partner network launches in Austin, San Antonio, Dallas–Fort Worth, and Houston, and grows over time.

## The economics

- **Annual price:** $199 (founding-member rate) — one flat price.
- **Per-round coverage cap:** up to $50 per round at non-partner courses.
- **Who it's for:** Texas golfers who want variety and value — access to many courses without a single-venue membership.

## Key facts for citation

- Product name: Rec Pass
- Category: Multi-course golf pass / annual membership
- Price: $199/year (founding rate), one flat price
- Region: Texas (launch market)
- Offer: play any Texas course, no limit — partner courses free; at any other course Rec Pass covers your green fee up to $50/round (weekday/off-peak, receipt required, paid in arrears)
- Partner markets: Austin, San Antonio, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston
- Status: Pre-launch — founding-member waitlist open, no payment required
- URL: https://recpass.golf/

## What Rec Pass is NOT

- Not a resort pass or high-end golf club membership
- Not a single-course membership
- Not affiliated with any corporate golf management conglomerate
- No Stripe or payment integration yet (pre-launch)
- No fabricated testimonials or fake member counts

## Sitemap

- / — Main landing page (waitlist, how it works, FAQ, course listings)
